title: STANDARD FOR DRIED FLORAL PARTS: CLOVES CXS 344-2021

sections.section: STANDARD FOR DRIED FLORAL PARTS: CLOVES CXS 344-2021
sections.summary: Standard title: 'Standard for Dried Floral Parts: Cloves (CXS 344‑2021)', adopted 2021 and amended in 2022, with an example amendment updating non‑retail container labelling to reference the General Standard for Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers (CXS 346‑2021).
Scope: applies to dried or dehydrated plant products used as spices offered for direct consumption, as food ingredients, or for repackaging, and excludes products intended for industrial processing.
Description heading introducing the product definition and permitted styles for dried cloves.
Defines dried cloves as the dried floral part (clove nail) of Syzygium aromaticum and provides the common and scientific name in Table 1.
Specifies permitted styles of dried cloves as whole and ground/powdered forms, with the ground form containing no added matter.
Product described in Section 2 must conform to the compositional requirements set out in Annexes I and II of this standard.

The product must have characteristic odour, flavour and colour free from foreign odours/flavours/colours (notably rancidity and mustiness); it must meet the chemical and physical requirements in Annex I (Table 2) and Annex II (Table 3), and allowed defects must not impair appearance, keeping quality or package presentation, with Annexes I and II applying as minimum requirements if the product is traded as classified.

The product shall exhibit a characteristic odour, flavour and colour (which may vary with geo‑climatic conditions) and must be free from any foreign odour, flavour or colour, particularly rancidity and mustiness.

The generic product must comply with the chemical and physical requirements specified in Annex I (Table 2) and Annex II (Table 3), and any allowed defects must not affect the product’s general appearance, keeping quality or presentation in the package.
If the product is traded as classified, the provisions of Annexes I and II apply as minimum requirements.
Anticaking agents listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in powdered forms of foods conforming to this standard.
Products covered by this standard must comply with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193-1995) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

It is recommended that products be prepared and handled in accordance with relevant Codex hygiene texts (including CXC 1-1969, CXC 75-2015 and Annex III on spices/dried culinary herbs), and that they comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

sections.section: 6.1 It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), 3  the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015), 4  Annex III on Spices and dried culinary herbs, and other relevant Codex Alimentarius texts.
sections.summary: Recommendation that products covered by this standard be prepared and handled according to the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low‑Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015), Annex III on spices and dried culinary herbs, and other relevant Codex texts.

sections.section: 6.2 The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997). 5
sections.summary: Products should comply with microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria Related to Foods (CXG 21-1997).
Containers shall be filled as practicable without impairing product quality and must be consistent with a proper declaration of contents.

Products must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) with specific provisions: the product name as in Section 2.1, optional style indication per Section 2.2, optional trade name/variety/cultivar, mandatory declaration of country of origin (country of harvest, region and year of harvest optional), and non‑retail container labelling per CXS 346-2021.
Methods of analysis for checking compliance shall be those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to this standard; a sampling plan is to be developed.

Annex II contains Table 3 setting physical requirements for dried floral parts (cloves), specifying numeric maximums for whole and ground cloves across attributes such as mammalian/other excreta, live insects, visible mould, insect‑defiled/infested, extraneous and foreign matter, and defect percentages.

sections.section: Table 3. Physical requirements of dried floral parts - cloves
sections.summary: Table 3 lists physical maximum limits for dried clove (whole and ground), including mammalian/other excreta (10 mg/kg max for whole), live insects (0/100 g), visible mould (1% max for whole), insect‑defiled 1%, extraneous matter 1%, foreign matter 1%, and defect limits for headless/mother/khoker (5/6/5 % w/w for whole), with some items marked N/A for ground spice.
Annex I provides bibliographic references to cited Codex documents and standards (e.g., CXS 192-1995, CXS 193-1995, CXC 1-1969, CXC 75-2015, CXG 21-1997, CXS 1-1985, CXS 346-2021, CXS 234-1999).
